summary: Linked several already-completed or near-completed objects by updating build status, section ownership, and symbol metadata. The PR promoted MSL/math_data.c, sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.c, hsd_3B27.c, and hsd_3B2B.c to Matching, added -Cpp_exceptions on for psappsrt and adjacent particle/psdisp objects, corrected psappsrt data declarations and symbol sizes/scopes, moved a .sdata2 range from hsd_40FF.c to hsd_3B34.c, split a bytecode/data blob into string labels, and fixed one float-vs-double literal in mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay. The decomp.dev bot reported +2428 linked-code bytes, +336 matched-data bytes, +1288 linked-data bytes, and 5 new matches, especially psappsrt extab/extabindex/.bss/.sbss and bytecode .data.

key_files.role: Changed object status to Matching for MSL/math_data.c, sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.c, hsd_3B27.c, and hsd_3B2B.c; added -Cpp_exceptions on to particle.c, psdisp.c, and psappsrt.c entries.
Adjusted extab/extabindex local labels, split HSD_ByteCode_804074F0 into individual string labels, corrected HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 size, and made PSAppSrt .sdata2 constants local @151/@152.
Moved .sdata2 range 0x804DEB10-0x804DEC00 from sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_40FF.c to sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c.
Changed HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958 from extern to static, defined HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 instead of declaring it extern, and reordered extern declarations.
Changed 30.4788F to 30.4788 in mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay so the expression uses a double literal after casting distance to f64.

what_changed: Marked MSL/math_data.c as Matching in configure.py.
Marked sysdolphin/baselib/psappsrt.c as Matching and compiled it with extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"].
Marked sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B27.c and hsd_3B2B.c as Matching.
Added -Cpp_exceptions on to particle.c and psdisp.c even though they remained NonMatching, matching the exception-table needs of the nearby baselib objects.
Moved ownership of .sdata2 start:0x804DEB10 end:0x804DEC00 from hsd_40FF.c to hsd_3B34.c in splits.txt.
Renamed several extab/extabindex placeholders from address-style names such as @etb_80005628 and @eti_8000584C to numeric local labels such as @141, @142, @153, and related labels.
Replaced one global HSD_ByteCode_804074F0 data object of size 0xA0 with multiple global string labels lbl_804074F0 through lbl_80407574.
Corrected HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 symbol size from 0x30 to 0x2C in .bss.
Changed HSD_PSAppSrt_804DEA68 and HSD_PSAppSrt_804DEA6C from global named float symbols to local @151 and @152 .sdata2 symbols.
In psappsrt.c, made HSD_PSAppSrt_804D7958 a static u16 array and emitted HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 as an actual HSD_ObjAllocData definition.
In mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay, removed the F suffix from 30.4788F, making the divisor a double literal.
smart_moves: Used build-configuration flips only after pairing them with symbol/split fixes, avoiding a superficial NonMatching-to-Matching change that would leave data sections unlinked.

Matched exception-support metadata by enabling -Cpp_exceptions on psappsrt and nearby particle/psdisp objects, which aligns with the bot report showing psappsrt extab and extabindex became 100% matched.
Recognized that HSD_ByteCode_804074F0 was not best represented as one opaque 0xA0 blob and split it into individual string symbols, yielding the reported bytecode .data completion.
Corrected symbol scope for psappsrt small data constants from global HSD_PSAppSrt_* names to local numeric labels, reducing false exported-symbol assumptions.
Fixed a literal-width mismatch in mndiagram.c with a one-character source change, a common matching tactic when an f64 expression should not contain a float-suffixed constant.
Adjusted BSS declaration/ownership in psappsrt.c and symbols.txt together: defining HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0 in C and correcting its symbol size to 0x2C supported the reported .bss match.
decomp_lessons: Before marking an object Matching, audit its split ownership, local-vs-global symbol scope, BSS sizes, and exception metadata; this PR required all of those.
Extab and extabindex completion can depend on per-object compiler flags such as -Cpp_exceptions on, not only on C source control flow.
If a data blob is almost matched but off by a small amount, inspect whether it is actually multiple strings or labels rather than one named object.
A source declaration changing from extern to a real definition can be the decisive step for linking BSS/SBSS data into the intended object.
Float suffixes matter: even when a value is cast through f64, a literal written as 30.4788F can produce different constants/code than 30.4788.
Splits.txt section ownership may need to move between adjacent baselib placeholder files as objects become linkable; do not assume historical section placement is final.
naming_conventions: Local generated labels may be represented as numeric @NNN symbols in symbols.txt when no meaningful global symbol name is warranted.
Address-style placeholder names such as @etb_80005628 and @eti_8000584C can be replaced by numeric local labels when matching recovered local exception metadata.
Use lbl_8040xxxx labels for anonymous data/string labels when a previous named object like HSD_ByteCode_804074F0 is too coarse.
Keep HSD_* names for real global baselib symbols such as HSD_PSAppSrt_804D10B0, but demote constants to local @ labels when evidence shows they are not global.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Enable -Cpp_exceptions on objects whose extab/extabindex sections need to be emitted and linked correctly.
Promote objects in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only after confirming code and data sections match.
Use precise literal typing to influence generated floating-point constants: 30.4788F and 30.4788 are not interchangeable in f64 math.
Represent BSS objects with the correct C storage duration: static for file-local arrays, non-extern definitions for data owned by the object.
Fix section assignment in splits.txt when a matched object pulls a section range that was previously attributed to a neighboring incomplete unit.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 progress from f3b89a0 to 8563b26: linked code 38.81% (+0.06%, +2428 bytes), matched data 38.80% (+0.03%, +336 bytes), and linked data 33.14% (+0.11%, +1288 bytes).
The bot listed 5 new matches: psappsrt extabindex +72, psappsrt .bss +48, psappsrt extab +48, psappsrt .sbss +8, and bytecode .data +2.
follow_up_queries: Find other NonMatching sysdolphin/baselib objects with extab/extabindex gaps that may need -Cpp_exceptions on.
Search for remaining HSD_ByteCode_* or large anonymous data blobs that should be split into string labels.
Audit HSD_PSAppSrt symbols for remaining incorrect global/local scope or BSS size assumptions.
Search for f64 expressions that divide by float-suffixed literals and compare against expected assembly.
Check neighboring hsd_3B2E, hsd_3A94, and hsd_40FF split ownership after hsd_3B27/hsd_3B2B/hsd_3B34 progress.
